name: Serverless & Data Engineer & Disaster
description: Personalized questions based on GitHub repositories

questions.question: Where does AWS Lambda execute by default?
choices: Inside Amazon EC2 instances managed by user
questions.explanation: Lambda runs in an AWS-managed, isolated execution environment by default, not inside a user-controlled VPC unless configured.

questions.question: What is AWS Lambda@Edge primarily used for?
choices: Running Lambda functions at CloudFront edge locations
questions.explanation: Lambda@Edge runs Lambda functions at CloudFront edge locations to reduce latency.

questions.question: What is a Lambda Function URL?
choices: A private internal VPC endpoint only
A built-in HTTPS endpoint to invoke Lambda directly
questions.explanation: Function URLs provide a simple HTTPS endpoint to invoke Lambda directly without API Gateway.

questions.question: What is a Lambda Layer?
choices: A ZIP archive containing reusable code or dependencies
questions.explanation: Lambda Layers are ZIP packages used to share libraries and dependencies across functions.

questions.question: When should you prefer containers over Lambda?
choices: When you need event-driven execution under 15 minutes
When execution exceeds Lambda limits or requires high memory (>10 GB)
When you want automatic per-request billing only
questions.explanation: Containers are better for workloads exceeding Lambda limits like long execution time or high memory needs.

questions.question: What is the maximum execution duration of AWS Lambda?
questions.explanation: Lambda maximum timeout is 15 minutes per invocation.

questions.question: Which scenario is NOT suitable for AWS Lambda?
questions.explanation: Lambda is not suitable for long-running tasks beyond its 15-minute limit.

questions.question: Which advantage do containers have over Lambda?
questions.explanation: Containers do not have the strict 15-minute execution limit of Lambda.

questions.question: Which workload is BEST suited for Lambda?
questions.explanation: Lambda is ideal for stateless microservices with variable traffic.

questions.question: What is a key cost consideration when using AWS Lambda?
choices: High invocation frequency can increase cost significantly
You pay only for idle time
questions.explanation: Lambda pricing is based on invocations and duration, so high traffic increases cost.

questions.question: Which service is commonly used for container orchestration in AWS?
questions.explanation: ECS and EKS are used for container orchestration.

questions.question: Which statement about Lambda networking is TRUE?
choices: Lambda always runs inside a VPC by default
Lambda runs outside VPC unless explicitly configured
Lambda only works inside private subnets
questions.explanation: Lambda runs in AWS-managed environment and can optionally be attached to a VPC.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of Lambda@Edge?
choices: Executes code closer to users for lower latency
questions.explanation: Lambda@Edge runs at CloudFront edge locations to reduce latency.

questions.question: Which Lambda feature helps reuse dependencies across multiple functions?
questions.explanation: Lambda Layers allow sharing code and dependencies across functions.

questions.question: What is a key disadvantage of Lambda compared to containers?
questions.explanation: Lambda has a 15-minute execution limit, unlike containers.

questions.question: Which architecture is best for migrating legacy on-prem applications to AWS?
questions.explanation: Containers are best for migrating existing monolithic or on-prem applications.

questions.question: Which feature allows direct HTTPS invocation of Lambda without API Gateway?
questions.explanation: Function URLs provide direct HTTPS access to Lambda.

questions.question: Which statement about Lambda cost is TRUE?
choices: Cost is based on reserved instances
Cost depends on execution time and number of requests
questions.explanation: Lambda pricing is based on requests and execution duration.

questions.question: Which workload is BEST suited for containers instead of Lambda?
choices: Batch processing with long execution time
questions.explanation: Containers are better for long-running batch processing workloads.

questions.question: What is a key characteristic of serverless computing like Lambda?
choices: You pay for idle compute capacity
You do not manage servers and scale automatically
questions.explanation: Serverless means no server management and automatic scaling.

questions.question: What is an Amazon ECS Cluster?
choices: A pool of compute resources where containers run (EC2 or Fargate)
questions.explanation: An ECS cluster is a logical grouping of compute resources (EC2 or Fargate) where containers run.

questions.question: What is an ECS Task Definition?
choices: A blueprint that defines container image, CPU, memory, ports, and environment variables
questions.explanation: A task definition is a blueprint describing how containers should run.

questions.question: What is an ECS Task?
choices: A running instance of a task definition
A Docker image stored in ECR
questions.explanation: A task is a running container based on a task definition.

questions.question: What is an ECS Service?
choices: A mechanism that maintains desired number of running tasks and handles scaling
questions.explanation: A service ensures desired task count, handles scaling, and replaces failed tasks.

questions.question: Which ECS launch type requires you to manage EC2 instances?
questions.explanation: EC2 launch type requires you to manage the underlying EC2 instances.

questions.question: Which ECS launch type is serverless?
questions.explanation: Fargate is serverless and AWS manages the infrastructure.

questions.question: What is the main role of an ECS Service?
choices: Run and maintain desired number of tasks
questions.explanation: Services ensure tasks are running and handle scaling and recovery.

questions.question: What happens if an ECS task fails in a Service?
choices: Service automatically replaces it to maintain desired count
Task is moved to another region
questions.explanation: ECS Service automatically restarts failed tasks.

questions.question: Which ECS component defines CPU, memory, and container configuration?
questions.explanation: Task definition defines compute resources and container settings.

questions.question: What is the difference between ECS Task and Task Definition?
choices: Task is blueprint, Task Definition is running container
Task Definition is blueprint, Task is running instance
Task is cluster, Task Definition is service
questions.explanation: Task definition is the template; task is the running instance.

questions.question: Which ECS component is responsible for scaling containers automatically?
questions.explanation: ECS Service handles auto scaling based on configuration.

questions.question: Which launch type is best when you want to avoid managing servers?
questions.explanation: Fargate is serverless and removes the need to manage EC2 instances.

questions.question: What does ECS Cluster NOT directly manage?
questions.explanation: ECS does not build images; that is handled by tools like Docker or CodeBuild.

questions.question: Which ECS component interacts with load balancers?
questions.explanation: ECS Service integrates with Elastic Load Balancing.

questions.question: What is a key benefit of ECS Fargate?
questions.explanation: Fargate is serverless, so AWS manages the infrastructure.

questions.question: What is a common use case for ECS?
questions.explanation: ECS is used to run containerized microservices.

questions.question: Which ECS component defines how many copies of a task should run?
questions.explanation: ECS Service defines desired task count.

questions.question: What happens when you update a Task Definition in ECS Service?
choices: All running tasks are automatically replaced with new version
questions.explanation: Updating service causes new tasks to be deployed with updated task definition.

questions.question: Which ECS concept is closest to a Docker container runtime instance?
questions.explanation: A task is a running container instance.

questions.question: What is the relationship between ECS Cluster and Tasks?
choices: Clusters store images, tasks store logs
Clusters provide compute capacity where tasks run
questions.explanation: Clusters provide the environment where tasks run.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of AWS Step Functions?
choices: To orchestrate workflows between distributed services
questions.explanation: Step Functions is used to orchestrate workflows across multiple AWS services.

questions.question: What happens if a step in AWS Step Functions fails?
choices: Workflow stops unless retry/catch is defined
questions.explanation: Step Functions stops on failure unless retry or catch logic is configured.

questions.question: Which AWS service is best for orchestrating ECS tasks in a workflow?
questions.explanation: Step Functions is designed to orchestrate ECS tasks and other AWS services.

questions.question: What is true about ECS Services?
choices: They maintain desired number of running tasks
questions.explanation: ECS Services ensure the desired number of tasks are always running.

questions.question: Can ECS Services directly coordinate with each other?
choices: No, they are independent and do not coordinate
questions.explanation: ECS Services are independent and do not coordinate with each other.

questions.question: Which AWS service allows waiting for a task to complete before starting another?
questions.explanation: Step Functions can wait for task completion before proceeding.

questions.question: What is a key limitation of ECS Services in workflow management?
choices: They do not support orchestration between services
questions.explanation: ECS Services manage tasks but do not orchestrate workflows.

questions.question: What is a Step Function state machine?
choices: A workflow definition describing steps and transitions
questions.explanation: A state machine defines workflow steps and transitions in Step Functions.

questions.question: Which feature allows Step Functions to retry failed tasks?
questions.explanation: Step Functions supports retry policies for handling transient failures.

questions.question: Which AWS service is responsible for maintaining running ECS tasks but NOT workflow orchestration?
questions.explanation: ECS Service manages task lifecycle but does not orchestrate workflows.

questions.question: Which Step Functions capability supports conditional branching?
questions.explanation: Choice states allow branching logic in workflows.

questions.question: What happens if Step Functions reaches a timeout?
choices: AWS retries the workflow automatically forever
questions.explanation: Step Functions execution fails if timeout is reached.

questions.question: Which combination is most common for workflow orchestration?
questions.explanation: Step Functions orchestrates ECS or Lambda tasks in workflows.

questions.question: What is a key difference between ECS Service and Step Functions?
choices: ECS Service orchestrates workflows, Step Functions runs containers
ECS Service manages tasks, Step Functions orchestrates workflows
questions.explanation: ECS Service maintains tasks; Step Functions orchestrates workflows.

questions.question: Which Step Functions feature helps handle error recovery?
questions.explanation: Retry and Catch provide error handling and recovery logic.

questions.question: What is the role of ECS Cluster in Step Functions orchestration?
choices: It provides compute capacity for running ECS tasks
questions.explanation: Cluster provides compute resources where ECS tasks run.

questions.question: Which service is best for coordinating multiple microservices execution order?
questions.explanation: Step Functions coordinates execution order across microservices.

questions.question: What is true about ECS Services in a distributed system?
choices: They communicate automatically with each other
They operate independently and do not manage workflow logic
questions.explanation: ECS Services are independent and do not coordinate workflows.

questions.question: Which Step Functions feature allows pausing between tasks?
questions.explanation: Wait states allow delaying execution in workflows.

questions.question: What is a main benefit of using Step Functions with ECS?
choices: It provides orchestration over independent ECS services
It eliminates the need for containers
It removes the need for clusters
questions.explanation: Step Functions orchestrates workflows across ECS services.

questions.question: What are the main stages of a data pipeline?
questions.explanation: A typical data pipeline includes ingesting, storing, processing, and analyzing data.

questions.question: What does ETL stand for in data engineering?
questions.explanation: ETL means Extract, Transform, Load where data is transformed before loading.

questions.question: What is the main characteristic of ELT?
choices: Data is loaded first, then transformed
Data is only streamed in real-time
questions.explanation: ELT loads raw data first and transforms it later inside the target system.

questions.question: When is ETL preferred over ELT?
choices: When you want raw data stored immediately
When you need structured, cleaned data before storage
questions.explanation: ETL is used when data must be cleaned and transformed before loading.

questions.question: What is batch processing?
choices: Processing data continuously in real time
Processing data periodically in large groups
questions.explanation: Batch processing handles data in scheduled intervals in bulk.

questions.question: What is streaming data processing?
choices: Processing data continuously as it arrives
questions.explanation: Streaming processes data in real time as it is generated.

questions.question: Which AWS service is commonly used for large-scale distributed batch processing?
questions.explanation: Amazon EMR is used for big data processing using frameworks like Hadoop and Spark.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of parallel processing in EMR?
choices: It speeds up processing by distributing work across nodes
It eliminates the need for data ingestion
questions.explanation: EMR parallel processing distributes computation across multiple nodes for efficiency.

questions.question: Which stage is NOT part of a typical data pipeline?
questions.explanation: Container deployment is not a standard data pipeline stage.

questions.question: What is the main goal of the ingest stage in a data pipeline?
choices: Collect and bring data into the system
questions.explanation: Ingestion is the process of collecting data from sources into the pipeline.

questions.question: Which AWS service is commonly used for data storage in pipelines?
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is widely used for scalable data storage in pipelines.

questions.question: What is a key difference between batch and streaming processing?
choices: Batch is real-time, streaming is delayed
Batch processes data periodically, streaming processes continuously
questions.explanation: Batch processes data in chunks; streaming processes continuously.

questions.question: Which type of processing is best for real-time analytics?
questions.explanation: Streaming is ideal for real-time analytics use cases.

questions.question: What is ELT commonly used for?
choices: Flexible analytics on raw data in data lakes
questions.explanation: ELT is used in modern data lakes where raw data is loaded first.

questions.question: Which AWS service is often used for real-time streaming ingestion?
questions.explanation: Amazon Kinesis is used for real-time streaming data ingestion.

questions.question: What is a key characteristic of a data engineer pattern?
choices: Building scalable pipelines for ingest, transform, and analyze data
questions.explanation: Data engineers design and build scalable data pipelines.

questions.question: Which system is best suited for distributed batch processing of big data?
questions.explanation: EMR is designed for distributed big data processing.

questions.question: What is stored first in an ELT pipeline?
questions.explanation: ELT loads raw data first before transformation.

questions.question: Which AWS service is commonly used for data transformation in ETL pipelines?
questions.explanation: AWS Glue is a serverless ETL service for data transformation.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of batch processing?
choices: Efficient processing of large volumes of data
questions.explanation: Batch processing is efficient for large-scale data processing workloads.
